body.body: Implement coordinated update across all running Igor instances: (1) PROC_SWARM_UPDATE habit: trigger=update all igors|pull latest|swarm update — broadcasts shutdown signal to all instances via cluster_ssh, waits for clean exits, runs git pull on each box, then restarts. (2) A simpler version: igor tool update_swarm() that SSH-executes the igor update loop on each node in machines.csv. Should handle: graceful exit (not kill -9), pull from origin/main, restart via igor alias. Deferred until cognition work is complete — ticket is a placeholder.
body.title: Swarm update procedure — exit all, git pull, restart all
body.result: Implemented D204 swarm update: (1) ssh_exec_all(windows_cmd, linux_cmd) primitive in cluster_ssh.py — iterates machines.json for SSH-capable online remote boxes, dispatches PowerShell on Windows, bash on Linux, skips local host; (2) update_swarm() tool — runs git pull + restart.flag glob on all boxes (remote via ssh_exec_all, local via direct subprocess), pull failure skips restart.flag and reports error, returns per-box audit log, forensic logs SWARM_UPDATE; (3) PROC_SWARM_UPDATE habit seeded (CP1, action, code_ref=tools.cluster_ssh:update_swarm, trigger=update swarm|pull latest|restart all boxes). seed script at claudecode/seed_swarm_update_habit.py.
